title: NQ Failed Overhead Capping In Ascent
focus_questions: Did the overhead wall actually stop the move or only pause it?
Was the pullback shallow enough to preserve continuation?

summary.thesis: Visible overhead liquidity can fail to stop a strong ascent when the pullback stays shallow and buyers reassert quickly into the same cap.
summary.operator_translation: Do not short every visible wall; judge whether the wall is actually winning.
summary.ai_usage_note: Prioritize this card when an overhead band exists but the trend keeps reloading beneath it and re-pressing higher.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: active ascent under visible overhead pressure. Required evidence: shallow pullback, preserved structure, and renewed buy aggression into the same band. Do not trade: if the pullback already broke the drive origin or the wall truly forced acceptance lower. Real trigger: quick reclaim and renewed push back into the cap. Failure point: deeper acceptance lower or lack of renewed lift.

when_relevant: Price is already in an upward campaign.
Visible sell liquidity remains overhead but fails to cause deeper damage.
Buyers quickly re-engage after a shallow pause.
required_evidence: pullback stays shallow relative to the prior drive
initiative_drive resumes upward after the pause
confirmation_signals: the pullback holds above the prior release area
buyers return quickly after the pause
price re-engages the same upper band with enough pressure to threaten another harvest attempt
invalidation_signals: pullback breaks the prior drive origin or key support
buyers fail to reclaim with urgency
the overhead band finally forces acceptance lower
no_trade_conditions: Do not fade the wall automatically before it proves it can force deeper acceptance lower.
Do not buy continuation if the pullback already damaged the prior drive origin or key support.
Do not keep adding long if the move is running into stronger higher-timeframe resistance with no renewed lift.

entry_archetypes.name: shallow pullback reclaim
entry_archetypes.description: Long after the shallow pullback holds and buyers reassert toward the same cap.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the reclaim is slow and structure remains vulnerable.

entry_archetypes.name: second push into cap
entry_archetypes.description: Long on the next organized push when the first cap attempt failed to break the ascent.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the market is already exhausting after multiple failed pushes.

management_notes: Track whether the cap is truly weakening or still producing damage.
Respect higher-timeframe resistance if the trend is already extended.
If the cap is finally harvested, switch attention to post-harvest logic.
review_questions: Did the wall actually stop the trend, or did it only pause it?
Was the pullback shallow enough to preserve continuation logic?
Did the long entry wait for renewed aggression, or was it placed too early?
